goals.g1: Is apply_compound's redirect-owner lookup (inherit-only, dropping when no inherit match) correct, or does it need a mapping_in fallback for merged-but-surviving inputs?

c1.statement: the end-to-end 3-match test's negation check flagged that redirect target equaled the original id in every case, unable to distinguish correct lookup from stale-fallback bug

c2.statement: hypothesized a mapping_in fallback for a possible 'merged but alive' input state, implemented it

c3.statement: tested compile_core directly: delete and inherit-values exhaustively partition all inputs, confirmed on a 2-input-1-output fan-in pattern -- no third surviving-but-not-inherited state exists

c4.statement: given c3, the mapping_in fallback would incorrectly resurrect a genuinely-deleted fan-in-loser -- reverted it

c5.statement: re-ran the original 3-match end-to-end test after reverting: identical correct result, no regression

c6.statement: constructed the actual drop-path case (crossing onto a fan-in-loser): consumed correctly includes it, resulting graph has zero edges -- crossing dropped cleanly, no resurrection

c7.statement: DECISION: the original inherit-only redirect lookup is correct as written. The investigated mapping_in fallback was empirically disproven and reverted. The DROP path is now validated by an executed test, closing the negation flagged after the first end-to-end run -- not by finding a substitution case, but by confirming none exists at this schema level (only inherit-unchanged or drop are possible outcomes).

derived_from.rule: the partition proof (c3) shows no third state is reachable, so the only two redirect outcomes are identity-preserved-via-inherit or dropped-via-delete, both now executed and confirmed correct

ans.statement: The original inherit-only redirect lookup was correct. A plausible mapping_in fallback was investigated, empirically disproven, and reverted. The DROP path is now validated end-to-end, closing the earlier negation.

k1.method: tested compile_core's partition property directly on a hand-built fan-in pattern
k1.result: confirmed exhaustive partition, no third state

k2.method: ran the actual drop-path scenario end-to-end
k2.result: crossing dropped cleanly, zero edges, correct consumed set

k3.method: check whether the original identity-substitution gap is resolved or just reframed
k3.result: resolved: no substitution case exists at this level; only inherit-unchanged or drop, both now validated

committed.statement: DECISION: the original inherit-only redirect lookup is correct as written. The investigated mapping_in fallback was empirically disproven and reverted. The DROP path is now validated by an executed test, closing the negation flagged after the first end-to-end run -- not by finding a substitution case, but by confirming none exists at this schema level (only inherit-unchanged or drop are possible outcomes).
